Goal_Statement: Increase the percentage of students meeting MAP reading growth projections from 58% to 68% school-wide.
Metric: % students meeting MAP reading growth projection
Key_Strategies: 1. Implement structured guided reading block K-5; 2. Launch Newsela nonfiction reading initiative 3-8; 3. Increase Lexia Core5 usage to 3x/week; 4. Monthly data meetings with grade-level teams
Mid_Year_Notes: Mid-year target met exactly at 62%. K-4 showing stronger growth (65%) than 5-8 (58%). Lexia usage has been inconsistent in some classrooms - need to reinforce expectations. Newsela adoption in middle school is going well.

Goal_Statement: Increase the percentage of students meeting MAP math growth projections from 52% to 62% school-wide.
Metric: % students meeting MAP math growth projection
Key_Strategies: 1. Implement daily number talks K-5; 2. Adopt problem-solving routines 6-8; 3. Increase DreamBox usage to 45 min/week; 4. Targeted algebra readiness intervention in grade 7-8
Mid_Year_Notes: Mid-year at 55% (target 56%) - close but slightly below. DreamBox usage dropped Oct-Dec and we are re-establishing expectations. Number talks implementation varies by teacher. Grade 7 math remains biggest concern area.

Goal_Statement: Reduce the achievement gap between Black students and White students in ELA proficiency on MCA from 26 points to 20 points.
Metric: MCA ELA proficiency gap (Black vs. White students)
Key_Strategies: 1. Culturally responsive teaching PD series (4 sessions); 2. Targeted tutoring for Black students below proficiency; 3. Review and revise curriculum for cultural relevance; 4. Increase representation in texts and materials
Mid_Year_Notes: CRT PD series on track (3 of 4 sessions complete). MAP winter data shows 23-point gap between Black and White student growth rates - slight improvement from fall. Targeted tutoring launched January for 12 students. Curriculum representation audit complete for ELA - identified gaps being addressed.

Goal_Statement: Improve student sense of belonging on Panorama SEL survey from 3.5 to 3.8 school-wide average (scale 1-5).
Key_Strategies: 1. Implement weekly advisory/community circles in all grades; 2. Launch peer mediation program in middle school; 3. Increase cultural celebration events; 4. Address LGBTQ+ inclusivity through GSA club
Mid_Year_Notes: Mid-year score unchanged at 3.5 - below target of 3.6. Middle school scores notably lower (3.1-3.2) pulling down the average. K-4 scores are stronger (3.7-4.2). Peer mediation program not yet launched (delayed). GSA club requested by students but not yet established - this is a priority.

Goal_Statement: Reduce disproportionality in office discipline referrals for Black students from 2.05 risk ratio to 1.5 or below.
Metric: Risk ratio for Black student ODRs vs. all students
Key_Strategies: 1. Implicit bias training for all staff; 2. Weekly referral data review by principal; 3. Restorative practices training to 100% of staff; 4. Implement de-escalation strategies training
Mid_Year_Notes: Risk ratio improved from 2.05 to 1.9 but not meeting mid-year target of 1.8. Implicit bias training completed December. Weekly referral review is happening and has prompted important conversations. Restorative practices training at 85% of staff (target 100% by May). Defiance/insubordination category remains most disproportionate.

Goal_Statement: Increase the percentage of teachers rated Proficient or Distinguished on Danielson evaluation from 82% to 90%.
Metric: % of teachers rated Proficient or Distinguished
Key_Strategies: 1. Intensive coaching for Developing-rated teachers; 2. Peer observation program; 3. Targeted PD based on observation data; 4. Mentor program for new teachers
Mid_Year_Notes: 3 teachers currently rated Developing (2 second-year teachers and 1 first-year). All receiving intensive coaching support. Two are showing growth; one (Mr. Foster) continues to struggle with classroom management. Peer observation program launched with positive feedback.

Goal_Statement: Increase family engagement survey satisfaction score from 4.0 to 4.3 (scale 1-5) and increase survey response rate from 55% to 70%.
Metric: Family survey satisfaction score and response rate
Key_Strategies: 1. Multilingual communication expansion; 2. ParentSquare optimization; 3. Quarterly family events (2 formal + 2 informal); 4. Parent leadership development
Mid_Year_Notes: Fall survey showed improvement in both satisfaction and response rate. Response rate gains driven by phone-based survey option in Spanish and Somali. Satisfaction gains in communication (+0.3) and teacher relationships (+0.2). Still need improvement in welcoming environment for families of color.

Goal_Statement: Increase fundraising revenue by 20% from $161K to $195K while diversifying revenue sources.
Key_Strategies: 1. Expand annual fund with matching gift program; 2. Grow gala revenue; 3. Apply for 4+ new grants; 4. Launch corporate partnership program
Mid_Year_Notes: Strong progress. Gala exceeded target. Annual fund on pace. Grant pipeline is active with 2 grants pending and 4 more planned for spring. Corporate matching program is new this year and showing promise.

Goal_Statement: Formalize technology refresh cycle and replace all devices older than 4 years.
Metric: % of student devices less than 4 years old
Key_Strategies: 1. Replace 30 iPad 8th Gen units; 2. Budget for Chromebook refresh cycle; 3. Inventory management system implementation; 4. Secure E-Rate and Title IV funding
Mid_Year_Notes: iPad replacement order placed but delayed due to procurement timeline. Expected delivery February. Once received this will jump to ~83%. Chromebook refresh budgeted for summer 2025. Inventory tracking improved with new spreadsheet system but need proper asset management tool.

Goal_Statement: Complete mid-cycle strategic plan review with updated metrics and community input.
Metric: Strategic plan review completed with stakeholder input
Key_Strategies: 1. Staff strategic plan review sessions (Aug and Jan PD); 2. Family focus groups; 3. Student focus groups; 4. Board retreat review; 5. Data analysis against strategic plan benchmarks
Mid_Year_Notes: Staff reviewed strategic plan in August PD and January mid-year session. Student focus group conducted January (middle school). Family focus groups planned for February-March in multiple languages. Board will review at March retreat. On track for May board approval of updated plan.
